content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 11 00 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
John Ruprecht 
324 N. Ma in St. 
P • 0 • Bo x 144 5 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Fort Bragg, CA 
95437 
Administration 
322·5660 
April 24, 1984 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6Ml 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-083 
Dear Mr. Ruprecht: 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of city 
Council candidate, Ann Waller. 
FACTS 
Ann Waller is running for a position on the Fort Bragg City 
Council. 
Ms. Waller is a reserve for the City's police 
department and her husband is an officer for the department. 
QUESTIONS PRESENTED 
1. 
Does the Political Reform Act prevent Ms. Waller from 
assuming a position on the City Council? 
2. 
If elected, will Ms. Waller be required to disqualify 
herself from decisions affecting the police department? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
The Political Reform Act will not prevent Ms. Waller 
from assuming a position on the City Council. 
2. 
If she is elected, Ms. Waller will not be required to 
disqualify herself on decisions affecting the police department. 


John Ruprecht 
Apr i 1 24, 1984 
Page 2 
DISCUSSION 
The Political Reform Act will not prevent Ms. Waller from 
serving as a City Councilwoman. 
However, the Act may affect her 
governmental decision making. 
Government Code Section 87100 11 
prohibits a public official from making, participating in the 
making, or using her official position to influence a 
governmental decision in which she knows or has reason to know 
she has a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial 
interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if 
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect on: 
(a) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official has [an] ••• investment worth more than one 
thousand dollars ($1,000). 
(b) 
Any r'eal property in which the public 
official has [an] ••• interest worth more than one 
thousand dollars ($1,000) • 
. (c) 
Any source of income ••• aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to, received by or promised to the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is 
made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official ••• holds any position of management. 
Section 87103. 
Generally the employers of an official and her spouse are 
sources of income to the official. 
(Section 82030.) 
However, 
in the present case, the police department is not a source of 
income because, under the Act, salary received from a local 
government agency is not considered to be "income.",fl 
(Section 82030 (b) (2) • ) 
Therefore, if Ms. Waller is elected, she 
will not be required to disqualify herself from decisions which 
will materially affect the police department. 
II Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
,fl 
I am assuming that Ms. Waller receives a salary as a 
police reserve officer. 


John Ruprecht 
April 24, 1984 
Page 3 
My advice is limited to the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
I cannot advise you as to whether there are any 
other provisions, outside of the Act, which will prevent 
Ms. Waller from serving as a City Councilwoman. 
If I can be of any further help to you, please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
Very truly yours, 
::J~ ~::;ue~ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
JSM:plh 


JOHN J. RUPRECHT 
c:I1tto'tiU.!J a.t La.w 
324 N. MAIN ST. 
P.O. BOX 1445 
FORT BRAGG. CA 95437 
707-964·2973 
April 2, 1984 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Secretary of State 
Political Reform Division 
1230 J Street 
P.O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, CA 
95807 
Dear Sir or Mada~: 
r r- : 
\.""- --
, 
. -""" o 
\ --
of 
I would like some information regarding a possible 
conflict of interest matter. 
If a candidate for City 
Council, who is also a reserve police officer with 
the City's Police Department, is elected, would that 
be an incompatible office under Government Code §1091 
et seq.? 
Ii elected, would the candidate have to disqualif~~ 
herself from any matters pertaining to the City Police ~ 
Department because (a) she is a reserve member of 
the City Police De?artment, and (b) her husband is 
also an officer in the City Police Department? 
Any information or assistance you can give me 
in this matter would be greatly appreciated. 
J,JR/ jlk 
Very truly yours, 
JOHN J. RUPRECHT 
City Attorney 
City of Fort Bragg
